mappings.rationaleAI: Cell membrane biology addresses membranes (A) including nature (A.1), compartmentalization (A.2), osmosis (A.3), diffusion (A.4), and active transport (A.5).
Neurophysiology addresses bioelectricity (B) and nerve impulse (C) including neuron structure (C.1), characteristics (C.2), impulse nature (C.3), and synaptic transmission (C.4).
Protein structures connect to membrane nature (A.1). Molecular machines relate to contractile activity (D).
Biomolecular structures connect to cell membrane (A) and nature (A.1).
Nervous system and motor responses connect to bioelectricity (B), nerve impulse (C), and muscle contraction (D).
Neural processes connect to nerve impulse (C) and synapses (C.4).
Retinal anatomy connects to neuron structure (C.1) and impulse nature (C.3).
Nervous systems and muscles connect to nerve impulse (C) and contractile activity (D.1).
Marine bioluminescent organisms connect to bioluminescence (E, E.2).
